event.summary: Recorded passing validation; acceptance remains a separate explicit transition.

acceptance.proof: StreamInfoImplementationVersionProviderOutput exposes no consuming into_parts method while its existing borrowed witness/version access remains unchanged.
StreamInfoImplementationVersionAcquisition retains exactly one consuming into_parts method required by X, and U/V accepted acquisition/value consuming access remains unchanged.
The accepted X receipt remains byte-identical and continues to describe only its original accepted head; Y owns separate current evidence.

The public-baseline-to-corrected-head diff is whitespace-clean and focused X/T/U/V/O/S, full owner, public-boundary/history, and workflow gates pass without dependency, feature, runtime, device, network, or authority expansion.

document.commit_policy: Commit one additive API-scope correction; do not amend, rebase, rewrite, force-push, or alter accepted LSLC-001X evidence.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Review X routing after removal of an unintended raw-output consuming escape; retain unchanged because accepted-acquisition composition and authority boundaries do not change.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review accepted instructions and run the full owner gate.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Review the public X summary after the API-scope correction; retain unchanged because the accepted composition claim remains the same.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review accepted router text and run the full owner gate.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Review public API scope and failure-preservation guidance; retain unchanged because current artifact/authority rules already require the narrower accepted-state escape and separate correction evidence.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review installed skill without public mutation.

non_scope: Any change to accepted acquisition into_parts behavior, U/V provider or acquisition APIs, X composition behavior, fixtures, documentation claims, accepted X receipts, earlier commits, or executed-push receipt.
Dependencies, features, Cargo/project/feature locks, unsafe, native libraries, provider calls, ambient acquisition, sockets, networking, endpoint semantics, devices, or runtime effects.
Protocol, wire, discovery, inlet, outlet, buffering, recovery, FFI, adapters, activation, or ecosystem compatibility.
Manifold admission, registry, subscription, route, lease, revision, replay, authorization, audit, session, topology, transport, command, product, or provider authority.
document.objective: Remove only the unintended consuming into_parts method from raw StreamInfoImplementationVersionProviderOutput, retain the required accepted-acquisition method, strengthen LSLC-001X validation against regression, preserve accepted X evidence byte-for-byte, and produce fresh exact Standard and no-force push evidence while recording the primary-review rejection and original compile failure.
outputs: Raw implementation provider output with borrowed access only and no consuming parts escape.
Accepted implementation acquisition retaining its required allocation-preserving into_parts method.
A focused X regression guard that distinguishes the raw output impl from the accepted acquisition impl.
Separate Standard and executed-push receipts preserving the primary rejection and earlier X compile failure.
